item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly surprised this one doesn't have a higher rating. It's the perfect mix of funny and disturbing. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Improv is a cult

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is this a reference to the Bojack Horseman episode "Yes And"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Quite possibly one of my favorite Series IV SCPs, love the ideas, can't really find anything negative to say. You earned your +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Trapped in improv theatre. Been there…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed this, its a unique concept, and I think you accomplish some good horror with your execution.

I do think that the 'Troupe' dialogue (monologue?) about Thespis at the end is far too on the nose. It is extremely straightforward. Other than that, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I hadn't remembered SCP-428 either, but this shares a lot of elements of SCP-2960 and SCP-332. The mechanics of keeping in the group and maintaining needs were ok, but you also used that description to deny them any individual character (e.g., they're always attending the scene). The only exception was the gunshot.

For what it adds beyond that… eh? The improv troupe name and dialogue aren't particularly interesting or inspiring, except for the dialogue in the last log, which was kind of amusing. It's not so much laughably bad (which would probably have been worse for the SCP) or particularly adept as just bland, and who needs more sub-par improv in thier lives/SCPs?

The attempts to be spooky and metaphysical with Thespis didn't particularly work. The discussion of Thespis just felt too matter-of fact, and the imagery in the last log was bland. I mean, you didn't even have them speak in ancient Greek, or some other mysteriousness. What's the point? Were they actually hijacked by the spirit of Thespis? Did they want this?

Also, I would say you could use some work on parsimony. You spent a fair amount of text ruminating on several of your concepts or images, and it almost always worked out for the worse. For example, I don't think much of the police note's length was useful, and the plain discussion of Thespis kind of accentuated the blandness.

So, bland imagery and improv, a bit lengthy, and some small twists on an idea executed much more effectively elsewhere. Gotta be -1

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: As I stated above, the image is my own. I took it using my phone.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Images used in posted articles must comply with the site's image policy. A source must be provided on the discussion page and the source must be compatible with the site's license. The author will be notified after this posting via Wikidot PM and will have 48 hours to properly source the image before it is edited out of the page.

Edit: must have misread, all is well

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Regardless, I like how self-contained this SCP is. Real good job man, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm going to assume you were blinded by the radiance of my comment, and that your comment was directed at it. :V

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I mean, "has elements of" basically means "made me think of" in this case. They're both a group of people who can't leave each other's proximity, otherwise honestly quite different.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you very much for your upvote and especially for your helpful feedback in the forums!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a pleasure to see this holding up nicely, I really like this idea. Original, thought out and just mysterious enough. +1 from me, obviously, and I hope the +1s keep coming.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An improv group is something I haven't seen explored in Foundation material before, but you brought it to the next level with an engaging discovery log and exploration of how the group survived before they were contained. I'm a little more neutral on the guard being incorporated into the group, but it's an interesting take on the give and take nature of improv; if the audience isn't willing to give an idea, it has to give something far greater. +1 from me, it's an example of an SCP that presents an interesting story without needing a sense that it's part of something larger.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, as far as I'm concerned, 'accompanied by' means whatever would make sense. Like all the members of the group move with the intention of going through a door, or cops/Foundation agents force them all through the door with the intention of getting them all through together. Basically the point is that the show doesn't stop, and they have to put it on together, because improv is about the whole troupe rather than any one person!

ETA: Also, before you posted this, I hadn't read SCP-428. I definitely see the similarities, but hopefully the improv element makes this different enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Overall, this was a splendid read; the last log really put this over the top. +1

ETA not sure why I replied, but whatever

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really cool. It's got elements of the Crowd, but it really is its own thing, with plenty of thought given into how these people survive in this state. Part of me wants to know what qualifies as 'accompanied by' (fixed that for ya, btw), but I don't think it would add anything to the article. This needs more upvotes!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really enjoyed this. Obviously completely aware of whats happening to them but totally unable to stop. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gaaah.

You made Nightmare Fuel out of improv comedy.

This is, among other things, the complete inverse of a thing what kills you. You will long for death, but the nightmare goes on forever.

Gaaah.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Spoiler in the collapsible.

+ spoiler
-spoiler

I do explain the additional member. It was added in Incident Log 3055-a, the last collapsible on the page, because a Suggestion Event means the troupe assimilates a new member.

Edit: that said, you're probably right. I'll take out most of them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm kinda neutral on this SCP, but could I suggest you take out this whole -7 -8 thing? There's no real mention or story as to how the seven members turned into eight post-initial containment, and I feel as though it makes the page look messy. I think just having a consistent amount of members would fit nicer, or if you want explain the final member.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My third SCP! The image is mine.
